edges.description: Insufficient propulsive motility produces episodes that mimic mechanical bowel obstruction in the absence of an occluding lesion.
Luminal stasis with accumulation of gas and fluid behind dysmotile segments distends the abdomen.
intermediate_mechanisms: Luminal gas and fluid accumulation from stasis

edges.description: Impaired colonic propulsion slows or prevents passage of stool.
Proximal gastrointestinal stasis and retained content promote emesis.
intermediate_mechanisms: Proximal stasis with retrograde transit of retained content

edges.description: Impaired transit and intolerance to enteral intake produce feeding difficulties.
intermediate_mechanisms: Enteral feed intolerance from impaired transit

edges.description: Chronic feeding intolerance and inadequate enteral nutrition lead to failure to thrive.

nodes.description: Abdominal distension from failed propulsive motility; often the most visible sign of obstructive episodes.

evidence.reference_title: Visceral myopathy diagnosed by a de novo ACTG2 mutation in a patient with chronic intestinal pseudo-obstruction-a case report.
evidence.snippet: The patient suffered from recurrent episodes of abdominal bloating

nodes.description: Severe constipation from impaired intestinal propulsion; may alternate with episodes of pseudo-obstruction.

Neuropathic CIPO arises from defects of the enteric nervous system — most commonly failed enteric-neuron development or maintenance. The flagship example is X-linked FLNA loss-of-function, in which the truncated filamin A protein fails to support proper enteric neuron development; ganglionitis and other primary neuropathic mechanisms also feed this node.

evidence.reference_title: Filamin A is mutated in X-linked chronic idiopathic intestinal pseudo-obstruction with central nervous system involvement.
evidence.snippet: the filamin N terminal region between the initial two methionines is crucial for proper enteric neuron development

nodes.description: Growth failure in pediatric CIPO from chronic malnutrition and inability to maintain adequate enteral intake.
Inability to tolerate enteral nutrition during active disease, often necessitating parenteral nutritional support.

evidence.reference_title: Visceral myopathy diagnosed by a de novo ACTG2 mutation in a patient with chronic intestinal pseudo-obstruction-a case report.
evidence.snippet: had feeding difficulties, and required long-term parenteral nutrition support

nodes.description: The shared physiological endpoint across CIPO subtypes is insufficient or absent propulsive intestinal motility, producing episodes that mimic mechanical obstruction. Upstream neuropathic (enteric-neuron developmental failure) and myopathic (smooth-muscle contractile failure) lesions converge on this endpoint.

evidence.reference_title: Pediatric Intestinal Pseudo-Obstruction: Progress and Challenges
evidence.snippet: Chronic intestinal pseudo-obstruction is a rare disorder and represents the most severe form of gastrointestinal dysmotility with significant morbidity and mortality.

nodes.description: Recurrent episodes that mimic mechanical bowel obstruction in the absence of physical luminal blockage. The defining clinical feature of the disease across all etiological subtypes.

evidence.reference_title: Chronic intestinal pseudo-obstruction in children and adults: diagnosis and therapeutic options.
evidence.snippet: Chronic intestinal pseudo-obstruction (CIPO) represents the most severe form of gastrointestinal dysmotility with debilitating and potentially lethal consequences

nodes.description: Myopathic CIPO arises from intrinsic smooth-muscle defects of the intestinal wall — poorly contractile or acontractile smooth muscle that cannot generate the propulsive contractions needed for peristalsis, regardless of the integrity of the enteric nervous system. Familial and sporadic visceral myopathies, as well as mitochondrial bioenergetic failure of smooth muscle in MNGIE, feed this node.

evidence.reference_title: Familial visceral myopathy diagnosed by exome sequencing of a patient with chronic intestinal pseudo-obstruction.
evidence.snippet: exome sequencing revealed an Arg148Ser mutation in the enteric smooth muscle actin gamma 2 (ACTG2) gene

nodes.description: Recurrent vomiting reflecting failed gastrointestinal transit during pseudo-obstructive episodes.
